title: ROUND 2: Remaining — @Sendable missing on detector protocol callbacks
description: Services/PauseConditionManager.swift: FocusStatusDetecting, CarPlayDetecting, DrivingActivityDetecting, and PauseConditionProviding callback closures (onFocusChanged, onCarPlayChanged, onDrivingChanged, onPauseStateChanged) cross actor boundaries but are not marked @Sendable. Round 1 added @MainActor to the classes but did not add @Sendable to the callback signatures. Under strict concurrency checking (Swift 6), these will produce warnings.

title: ROUND 2: Remaining — OverlayView writes UserDefaults directly
description: Views/OverlayView.swift line 236: UserDefaults.standard.set(true, forKey: AppStorageKey.openSettingsOnLaunch) is a direct persistence write from the View layer. Survives Round 1 — the analytics closure injection was done (onAnalyticsEvent) but this UserDefaults write was not extracted. Move to the onDismiss callback chain in OverlayManager or AppCoordinator so the View has zero persistence dependencies.

title: ROUND 2: New — PauseConditionManager.startMonitoring() re-entry accumulates Combine subscriptions
description: Services/PauseConditionManager.swift line 222: startMonitoring() appends to cancellables via .store(in:) (lines 247, 258) without clearing existing subscriptions first. If startMonitoring() is called twice without stopMonitoring(), the old sinks remain active and duplicate updates fire. Add cancellables.removeAll() at the top of startMonitoring() before subscribing, mirroring the cleanup in stopMonitoring() (line 275).

title: ROUND 2: Remaining — Hardcoded opacity values bypass AppOpacity enum
description: 4 locations still use raw opacity values despite AppOpacity tokens existing with identical values: (1) DesignSystem.swift:317 .opacity(0.65) → AppOpacity.subtleBorder, (2) DesignSystem.swift:322 .opacity(0.10) → AppOpacity.warningBackground, (3) OnboardingPermissionView.swift:116 .opacity(0.72) → AppOpacity.mutedTimestamp, (4) YinYangEyeView.swift:52 .opacity(0.65) → AppOpacity.subtleBorder. Round 1 fixed OverlayView and SettingsView but missed these 4.

title: ROUND 2: New — ReminderScheduler lacks explicit @MainActor despite @MainActor protocol
description: Services/ReminderScheduler.swift line 59: final class ReminderScheduler: ReminderScheduling conforms to @MainActor protocol ReminderScheduling but the class itself is not annotated @MainActor. Protocol conformance isolates the protocol methods, but the private stored property (notificationCenter, line 71) and private enum (NotificationID) are not actor-isolated. The init can be called from any isolation context. Add explicit @MainActor to the class declaration for consistency and to prevent future non-protocol members from escaping isolation.
